commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

issues.description: Hook name mismatch: 'ui.tsx' calls 'useWeather' but 'hooks.ts' defines 'useComponent', causing a runtime crash.
issues.fix: Rename 'useComponent' to 'useWeather' in hooks.ts or update the import/call in ui.tsx.

issues.description: The provided code for 'ui.tsx' is truncated and incomplete, missing the closing tags and the 'ForecastStrip' invocation.
issues.fix: Complete the 'ui.tsx' file by adding the ForecastStrip component and closing the Stack, Box, Card, and Container tags.

issues.description: Emojis used for weather conditions lack ARIA labels or 'aria-hidden' attributes.
issues.fix: Wrap emojis in a span with 'role="img"' and an 'aria-label', or use 'aria-hidden="true"' if the text condition is nearby.

issues.description: The 'WeatherDetails' component contains a dummy '_unused' variable to satisfy an import requirement, which is poor practice.
issues.fix: Remove the unused variable and properly use the imported type in the function signature or remove the redundant import.

evaluations.critique: The visual hierarchy and use of design tokens are excellent, creating a professional-looking weather card. However, the component is currently broken due to a hook naming mismatch and a truncated main file, and it lacks basic ARIA attributes for the decorative emojis.

evaluation.critique: The component properly uses design tokens for colors and spacing, renders forecast data from props correctly, and has good visual hierarchy. However, the layout has a truncated ForecastStrip component (code cuts off mid-word), and polish is limited by missing hover/focus states on interactive elements and no loading/error states.

violations: Hardcoded string "Sunny" found — should be a prop default

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

issues.description: The category color logic is duplicated and inconsistent across files. ElementCell uses hyphenated keys (e.g., 'alkali-metal'), while Legend and constants use spaces (e.g., 'alkali metal').
issues.fix: Consolidate category mapping into a single utility function or use the map defined in constants.ts across all components.

issues.description: Interactive grid cells are implemented as Box/div elements without roles, tabIndex, or keyboard event listeners.
issues.fix: Add role="button", tabIndex={0}, and an onKeyDown handler to ElementCell to support keyboard navigation.

issues.description: The CATEGORY_COLOR_MAP and CATEGORY_LABELS in constants.ts are defined but never imported or used by the components.
issues.fix: Import and use these constants in ElementCell and Legend to ensure a single source of truth.

issues.description: Hover states are missing visual feedback beyond the cursor change.
issues.fix: Add a brightness filter or a slight background color shift on hover to the ElementCell.

evaluations.critique: The visual structure and layout of the periodic table are well-executed, with good use of CSS Grid and scale transitions for selection. However, the code quality is hampered by significant redundancy and a logic mismatch in category naming that would break the color coding. Accessibility is also below standard as the table is not keyboard-navigable.

commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

issues.description: The root WeatherCard component that composes all the sub-components is completely missing from the provided code.
issues.fix: Implement the main WeatherCard component that passes props down to WeatherHeader, WeatherMain, WeatherDetails, and ForecastStrip.

issues.description: The source code for ForecastItem.tsx is truncated mid-line, and the final assembly is missing.
issues.fix: Complete the ForecastItem component and provide the main component file.

issues.description: The useWeather hook is defined but not utilized; instead, logic for condition icons and formatting is duplicated inside WeatherMain.
issues.fix: Centralize formatting logic within the useWeather hook and consume it in the UI components.

evaluations.critique: The atomic components (Header, Main, Details) demonstrate excellent visual hierarchy and strict adherence to the design system's tokens. Accessibility is a strong point with semantic roles and ARIA labels, but the submission is fundamentally incomplete without the main WeatherCard assembly and due to truncated code.

evaluation.critique: The component has solid data handling and clear visual hierarchy, but suffers from inconsistent design token usage (hardcoded fallback values like '8px', '16px' in inline styles), missing interactive polish (no hover/focus states on forecast items), and an incomplete ForecastStrip component truncation that breaks the implementation.

violations: Hardcoded string "Sunny" found — should be a prop default

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel

Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

issues.description: Logic error in search highlighting: 'isHighlighted || !isHighlighted' is a tautology that always evaluates to true, preventing the intended dimming of non-matching elements.
issues.fix: Change the opacity logic to 'opacity: isHighlighted ? 1 : 0.4' or similar.

issues.description: Interactive element cells are implemented using 'div' tags without 'tabIndex', keyboard event listeners, or ARIA roles.
issues.fix: Wrap element content in a <button> or add tabIndex={0}, role='button', and an onKeyDown handler to the div.

issues.description: The Periodic Table grid has a fixed 18-column layout that may cause horizontal overflow issues on smaller screens without a clear indicator to scroll.
issues.fix: Add a visual scroll indicator or a 'Scroll to explore' hint for mobile users.

evaluations.critique: The component successfully implements the complex 18-column grid layout required for a periodic table and handles selection state well. The detail panel and search functionality provide a good user experience, though accessibility is currently a weak point due to non-semantic interactive elements. Fixing the boolean logic in the opacity style will significantly improve the utility of the search feature.

evaluation.critique: The component demonstrates solid use of design tokens and proper grid layout for the periodic table, with good data presentation from props. However, there are notable issues: the grid uses hardcoded minmax pixel values (40px, 48px) instead of pure token-based spacing, some inline styles lack token consistency, and the search input and legend sections are incomplete in the provided code (appears truncated).
